title: Improve page load performance: optimize FCP and LCP

requirements.summary: Implement non-blocking Google Fonts loading with preconnect hints to eliminate render-blocking font requests and improve First Contentful Paint
acceptanceCriteria: Google Fonts stylesheet is loaded asynchronously and does not block rendering
preconnect hints for fonts.googleapis.com and fonts.gstatic.com are present in <head> before the fonts link
A <noscript> fallback link for the fonts stylesheet is present

file_operations.description: Add <link rel='preconnect'> tags for fonts.googleapis.com and fonts.gstatic.com before the Google Fonts stylesheet link. Convert the existing Google Fonts <link rel='stylesheet'> to use media='print' onload="this.media='all'" for async loading. Add a <noscript> fallback with the original stylesheet link to ensure fonts load when JavaScript is disabled. These changes eliminate render-blocking font requests that delay FCP.

requirements.summary: Add loading='eager' and fetchpriority='high' to the hero background image to ensure the Largest Contentful Paint element loads with highest priority
acceptanceCriteria: The hero background image is loaded eagerly with high fetch priority
The LCP image is not lazy-loaded
The hero section visual appearance is unchanged

file_operations.description: Locate the hero background image element (currently rendered as a CSS background-image or inline style). Convert it to an tag with loading='eager' and fetchpriority='high' attributes, positioned absolutely behind the hero content using Tailwind classes (absolute, inset-0, z-0, object-cover). Reference the generated hero image at frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-bg.dim_1920x1080.png. Ensure the existing hero content remains above the image (relative z-index). This ensures the LCP element is fetched with highest priority and not lazy-loaded.

requirements.summary: Add loading='lazy' to all below-the-fold images to prevent off-screen images from downloading during initial page load, improving FCP and LCP
acceptanceCriteria: All service card images have loading='lazy'
No below-the-fold images trigger network requests on initial load
Hero image remains loading='eager' with fetchpriority='high'

file_operations.description: Add loading='lazy' attribute to all six service card elements (service-refection-toiture.dim_800x500.png, service-pose-tuiles.dim_800x500.png, service-reparation.dim_800x500.png, service-isolation.dim_800x500.png, service-zinguerie.dim_800x500.png, service-nettoyage.dim_800x500.png). This prevents below-the-fold service images from downloading during initial page load.
Review all elements in the About section. If any images are present and do not already have loading='lazy', add the attribute to defer loading until the user scrolls near the section.
Review all elements in the Testimonials section. If any images are present and do not already have loading='lazy', add the attribute to defer loading until the user scrolls near the section.
Review all elements in the Footer section. If any images are present (logos, icons) and do not already have loading='lazy', add the attribute to defer loading until the user scrolls to the footer.

requirements.summary: Preload the hero/LCP image in the HTML head to start fetching it before CSS and JS processing, improving Largest Contentful Paint
acceptanceCriteria: A <link rel='preload' as='image'> tag for the hero image is present in <head>
The href matches the actual hero image path used in Hero.tsx
The preload tag appears early in <head>, before render-blocking resources

file_operations.description: Add <link rel='preload' as='image' href='/assets/generated/hero-bg.dim_1920x1080.png' /> inside <head>, positioned early (after charset/viewport but before stylesheets). This tells the browser to immediately fetch the hero image (frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-bg.dim_1920x1080.png) as soon as the HTML is parsed, before CSS and JS processing, improving LCP timing.

requirements.summary: Inline critical above-the-fold CSS in the HTML head to reduce render-blocking time and improve First Contentful Paint
acceptanceCriteria: Critical CSS for hero and navigation is inlined in a <style> block in <head>
The page renders its above-the-fold content without waiting for the full CSS bundle
No visual regression — all sections still appear correctly styled

file_operations.description: Extract critical above-the-fold CSS from frontend/src/index.css and inline it in a <style> tag inside <head>. Include: font-face declarations for the primary font stack, CSS custom properties (color tokens from :root), base hero section styles (positioning, typography, background), and navigation styles (positioning, background, transitions). Place the <style> block after meta tags but before external CSS links. This allows the browser to render visible content immediately without waiting for the full CSS bundle, improving FCP.

requirements.summary: Add lang attribute, DNS prefetch hint, and cache control meta tag to optimize resource loading and improve FCP/LCP
acceptanceCriteria: A dns-prefetch hint for fonts.googleapis.com is present in <head>
No existing meta tags are removed or broken

file_operations.description: Ensure the <html> tag has lang='fr' attribute (add if missing). Add <link rel='dns-prefetch' href='https://fonts.googleapis.com' /> in <head> to speed up DNS resolution for Google Fonts. Add <meta http-equiv='Cache-Control' content='max-age=31536000, immutable' /> to optimize caching of static assets. Do not remove or modify existing meta tags (Google site verification, canonical, Open Graph, JSON-LD structured data).
